mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days via downward earnings revisions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts generate a joint demand contraction signal that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, capturing household balance sheet stress not visible in either series alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST has rank correlation with 20-day forward returns below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days only when the federal funds rate exceeds 4%, reflecting supply weakness amplified by restrictive monetary policy; the signal is absent during easing cycles.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, INDPRO month-over-month declines fail to produce negative average 5-day returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales of more than one percent predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as demand shocks trigger rapid near-term earnings repricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative following month-over-month RSAFS declines exceeding 1% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of declining nonfarm payrolls and a rising unemployment rate predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because the interaction captures accelerating labor market deterioration more reliably than either series in isolation.
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises fail to predict negative 60-day returns in 60% or more of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding the federal funds rate predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days by compressing real corporate margins and forcing future monetary tightening; the signal is inactive when real rates are positive.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL exceeds FEDFUNDS, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because aggregate demand expansion drives top-line revenue growth across the index.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.
